yesterdayStoryline.headline: Dramatic finales reshape Groups J, K and L
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Late goals and VAR rulings decide qualification; England top Group L, DR Congo reach knockouts.

todayStoryline.headline: South Africa meet Canada to open the knockout stage
todayStoryline.subtitle: Canada arrive favourites if Davies is fit; South Africa seek first World Cup knockout win.

lines: England's second-half goals ended a frustrated stalemate in Group L.
The victory books England top of Group L and a safer knockout route.

yesterday.scorers: P. Sucic 31' (CRO) · D. Luckassen 73' (GHA) · N. Vlasic 83' (CRO)

lines: Croatia's late Vlasic header settled a tight Group L contest.
Victory earns Croatia runners-up spot and a tougher last‑32 draw.
A tense stalemate produced few clear chances and disciplined defending.
Colombia top Group K; Portugal progress as runners-up after the draw.

yesterday.scorers: E. Shomurodov 10' (UZB) · F. Mayele 78' (COD) · Y. Wissa 68' (p), 90+1' (COD)

lines: DR Congo staged a second-half comeback sparked by Wissa's brace.
The victory secures DR Congo's first knockout berth and an England tie.

yesterday.scorers: G. Lo Celso 19' (ARG) · L. Martínez 31' (pen) (ARG) · M. Al‑Taamari 55' (JOR) · L. Messi 80' (ARG)

lines: Argentina rotated personnel but controlled early, Lo Celso and Martínez scored.
Messi's substitute free-kick added gloss; Argentina finish top of Group J.

yesterday.scorers: M. Arnautović 28' (AUT) · R. Belghali 45' (ALG) · M. Sabitzer 55' (AUT) · R. Mahrez 60', 90+3' (ALG) · S. Kalajdžič 90+6' (AUT)

lines: Algeria and Austria produced a six-goal thriller decided in heavy stoppage time.
Both teams qualify from Group J after a chaotic, dramatic finale.

facts.home: South African fans made the vuvuzela synonymous with World Cup stadium noise in 2010.
facts.away: Alphonso Davies was born in a Ghanaian refugee camp before moving to Canada aged five.

news.headline: Drone and artillery strikes kill civilians on Russia‑Ukraine border
news.body: Drones and artillery strikes killed civilians on both sides of the Russia‑Ukraine border on Saturday, local officials said. The incidents heightened fears of escalation and added pressure to diplomatic efforts in the region.

footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs
